integration.title: Seamless Integration

questions.question: How does patient scheduling work across multiple locations?
questions.answer: Our cross-location scheduling system maintains a unified view of all provider availability across your entire practice group. Front desk staff can schedule patients at any location from a single interface, with access to the complete schedule. The system allows for location-specific rules (such as different hours or provider schedules) while maintaining a centralized view. Patients can also be transferred between locations while maintaining their complete records and history.

questions.question: Can we maintain different fee schedules for different locations?
questions.answer: Yes, the platform supports location-specific fee schedules to accommodate different market conditions and patient demographics. You can maintain separate fee schedules for each location while still generating consolidated financial reports at the group level. The system also allows you to implement universal fee changes across all locations when needed, with appropriate permissions and approval workflows to control these changes.

questions.question: How does the system help standardize clinical protocols?
questions.description: The platform includes a clinical protocol management module that allows you to create, distribute, and monitor standardized procedures across all locations. You can define protocols for common procedures, create standardized treatment plans, and share clinical best practices. The system tracks adherence to these protocols and highlights variations between locations, helping you maintain consistent quality of care regardless of which office a patient visits.

questions.question: What kind of performance comparisons can we make between locations?
questions.answer: Our analytics dashboard enables comprehensive performance comparisons across all metrics, including production, collection, new patients, case acceptance, and more. You can compare locations side-by-side or against group averages, with the ability to normalize data based on factors like location size, provider count, or operational hours. The system automatically highlights significant variations and trends to help identify both underperforming locations and best practices that can be replicated.

questions.question: How does the system handle the addition of a new location?
questions.answer: The platform includes dedicated tools for practice onboarding and integration. When adding a new location, the system guides you through a structured process including data migration, staff training, and workflow implementation. For acquisitions, we provide tools to map existing systems to your standardized procedures and gradually transition the new office to your protocols. Most new locations can be fully integrated within 2-4 weeks, with minimal disruption to patient care.

reviews.name: Dr. Robert Johnson
reviews.comment: Managing multiple locations used to require a full-time administrator for every 2-3 offices. With this platform, we've centralized operations and now run 8 locations with the same administrative team that previously handled 3. The standardized protocols have improved our clinical outcomes, and the cross-location scheduling has virtually eliminated downtime across all practices. Our group production is up 27% year-over-year.

reviews.name: Dr. Jennifer Martinez
reviews.comment: The acquisition integration tools have been invaluable as we've grown through practice purchases. We've been able to bring new offices fully into our system within 30 days, compared to the 3-6 months it took previously. The ability to compare performance metrics between locations has helped us identify and address underperforming offices quickly. We've seen a 22% improvement in our consolidated collection ratio since implementation.

reviews.name: Michael Thompson
reviews.comment: As a DSO managing practices across three states, this platform has been transformative for our operations. The centralized inventory management alone has saved us over $200,000 annually by optimizing supply purchases and enabling transfers between locations. The standardized workflows have reduced training time for new staff by 40%, and our ability to shift providers between locations as needed has significantly improved production efficiency.
